ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Phillips, via Zoom, for the San Francisco Public Guardian (SFPG), Trustee; Andrew Schultz, via Zoom, court appointed attorney for Craig S. Porter, Beneficiary.

Ms. Phillips reports of not planning to file a petition to request fees in the related conservatorship matter but advises that it will follow whichever protocol the court provides. The court mentions the special needs trust expense regarding massages and confirms with Ms. Phillips that those expenses have been added to this accounting period. Mr. Schultz reports that his client has advised that he would like new shoes and a bed and that the SFPG is aware of his client's request. The court advises Ms. Phillips that it is inclined to allow trustee fees on the hours spent on this matter at an hourly rate of $145. Ms. Phillips offers testimony regarding previous fee requests being granted on a one percent value of the trust. Mr. Schultz advises that he has spoken to his client regarding the SFPG's fee request, but that Mr. Porter did not offer a reaction. Mr. Schultz reports that Mr. Porter was alert and conversational during a recent conversation with him. Ms. Phillips confirms that Mr. Porter remains at Laguna Honda Hospital.

After brief, additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kahler. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-11-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sally Gratz for the San Francisco Public Guardian.

The Court asked if it is possible to deposit the money in another location with higher interest. Ms. Gratz stated that it is safe in this account, that the Public Guardian does not want to start investing as it is costly to maintain and that the Public Guardian feels that as itself and a government agency it needs an extra layer of protections. The Court stated that there must be other options that are safe. Ms. Gratz stated that if you have a big lump of case it needs to be kept safe and accessible, that they also have a duty to keep the money available, that when they asked for advice they were told to keep it in a savings account, that there is difficulty in hiring a broker as to who would pay and be responsible for the broker, that if money is already in a brokerage account they leave it there and that she can talk to the Public Guardian regarding other options. The Court stated that it does not mean to encourage risk or complexity. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
